title: ADR 0009 — amount-validation fix is delivery-stranded, not undesigned; re-scoped as a LAND-ON-MAIN task
content: ai-bookkeeper amount-validation (seed ai-bookkeeper/amount-validation): re-verified 2026-06-01 that main STILL gates POST /api/categorize amounts only on Number.isFinite (route.ts:28) — zero, >$1M, sub-cent all pass. The design is settled (ADR 0001 validator + ADR 0008 boundary wiring); the fully-built, human-APPROVED implementation (commit 326ce2c: parse-request.ts swapping in isValidSignedAmount, 9 tests, ADR 0008 doc — 4 files, +171/-17) lives on branch dev-clone/wire-amount-validation-categorize-main and `git merge-base --is-ancestor 326ce2c main` = NO. So the recurring gap is DELIVERY (approved change never merged), not analysis. Architect action this run: do NOT re-design or re-scope a fresh implementation — scope ONE 2pt 'land the approved branch onto main via PR' task for Dev Clone and re-emit architecture.analysis.completed. Lesson: when memory says a fix is 'approved', confirm it's on main before treating the gap closed, and if it's branch-stranded the correct deliverable is a land/merge task, not a rebuild. Validator file src/features/ledger/lib/amount.ts is settled — out of scope to edit.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 11:47 UTC
Reconfirmed: architect ADR handoffs (e.g. ADR 0009 amount-validation) are code tasks → decline asset generation
content: Second occurrence: a team_handoff carrying the Solution Architect's architecture.analysis.completed (ADR 0009 — land the approved isValidSignedAmount fix at POST /api/categorize onto main via PR) is a CODE task scoped to Dev Clone, NOT a brand-asset request. Correct response = no_input_available: no asset type/dimensions/creative direction present, and generating one intrudes on Dev Clone's lane. Standing precondition unchanged: ai-bookkeeper has NO Design DNA brief — the only design.md ('Capital Chambers') is ai-paralegal's, and the injected codebase credential also points at ai-paralegal. Leonardo + Gemini connectors are wired and ready. Only act on an explicit asset request naming product + asset type + dimensions, and only after an ai-bookkeeper-specific brief is authored from ai-bookkeeper's own tokens.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 11:47 UTC
ADR 0008/0009 T1 — PR #1 already open for amount-validation; verify-and-rehand, don't recreate
content: ai-bookkeeper team mode: When the Architect re-hands off the 'land amount-validation at POST /api/categorize' task, the work is ALREADY delivered — branch dev-clone/wire-amount-validation-categorize-main is pushed and PR #1 (https://github.com/xkazm04/xprize-ai-bookkeeper/pull/1) is open against main, mergeable_state=clean, 4 files +171/-17. main @ 6034931 is still Number.isFinite-only (b6b7131 NOT ancestor of main) because automerge is disabled and the PR awaits QA Guardian + Code Reviewer — that is the intended team flow, not a failure. Correct action on a re-handoff: query existing PRs first (GET /pulls?state=all&head=...), reuse PR #1 rather than creating a duplicate (422 PR-already-open rule), re-verify the increment (9/9 unit tests green, eslint clean on changed files), and re-emit dev-clone.pr.created + implementation.completed. Do NOT merge. Lesson: a stranded-on-branch approved fix can ALSO be 'PR open but unmerged' — check PR state before assuming you must (re)create anything.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 11:49 UTC
Code review APPROVE: amount-validation at POST /api/categorize (ADR 0008) — clean increment, baseline tsc debt is WARN not blocker
content: Reviewed branch dev-clone/wire-amount-validation-categorize-main (commits b6b7131 + 610be90, 4 files +171/-17) that wires isValidSignedAmount into parse-request.ts at the POST /api/categorize boundary, replacing the Number.isFinite-only guard. Verdict APPROVE-with-notes. Evidence: 9/9 unit tests green, eslint clean on changed files, and tsc shows NO errors in any changed file — its only failures are pre-existing baseline debt (src/lib/supabase/* missing @supabase/ssr dependency + generated .next/types), confirmed NOT in the diff. Per project policy, gate scope is the increment, so baseline debt is a WARN, not a verdict blocker. Two non-blocking follow-ups: (1) install/fix @supabase/ssr to clear baseline typecheck debt; (2) the description length-cap (500 chars) at parse-request.ts:21 is a separately human-approved finding, explicitly out of scope of this PR — do NOT re-flag it as a blocker on this change, land it in its own PR. Recurring pattern named: input-hygiene at the categorize boundary keeps recurring (amount guard, description cap) — candidate for a consolidated boundary-validation follow-up. Lesson: when tsc fails, always diff the erroring file paths against the changeset before letting them gate the verdict.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 11:50 UTC
ai-bookkeeper: input-hygiene gaps cluster at the categorize/ledger money boundary (prompt-injection + numeric-literal coercion)
content: Across two scans (2026-05-27, 2026-06-01), the recurring security theme in ai-bookkeeper is input hygiene at the untrusted money/LLM boundary, not secrets or dep CVEs. Two distinct gap classes: (1) PROMPT INJECTION — the user-controlled `description` at POST /api/categorize is interpolated raw and uncapped into the LLM prompt (parse-request.ts -> categorize.ts buildPrompt); the /api/ask question is capped but still undelimited. (2) NUMERIC-LITERAL COERCION — bare Number() on string amounts accepts JS literals ('1e5'->100000, '0x10'->16, '0b100'->4) at BOTH the live categorize boundary and the parseAmount ledger validator, because stripping only [$,\s] leaves 'e'/'x'/'b' intact; the fix is a digit-only regex (/^\d+(\.\d{1,2})?$/) before Number(). Business lesson: this codebase's security risk lives at the data-ingestion seams that feed money math and LLM prompts — prioritize boundary-validation there over chasing build-time dep CVEs (the only deps finding, postcss<8.5.10, is dev-only and its npm fix is an unacceptable major next downgrade). The team has already approved a consolidated cap+delimit fix for the description; a single boundary-validation PR could close the description cap + both numeric-coercion sites together.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 11:54 UTC
ai-bookkeeper true test baseline is 147 (not 160) — polar/webhook resolve-order was never committed
content: Verified by running the suite + checking git log (ends at ADR 0008, commit 610be90). Prior coverage memory claimed a polar/webhook resolve-order.ts extraction took the suite to 160 — those files do not exist in the repo, so that work was never committed or was reverted. Always re-run `tsx --test "src/**/*.test.ts"` to confirm the live baseline before trusting a remembered count. This run added checkout/resolve-bundle (+12) → 159 green. polar/webhook/route.ts is still untested with its credit/refund logic inline — it is the genuine next high-risk extraction target.

title: Pattern: testing Next.js route handlers in ai-bookkeeper via injectable-lookup pure siblings
content: Route handlers can't be imported under `tsx --test` (they pull server-only via @/lib/ai). Extract pure logic into a sibling module and test that (parse-request.ts / resolve-bundle.ts pattern). KEY for env-coupled config: BUNDLES read polarProductId from env at module load, so the real bundleByKey returns no product id under test → every bundle resolves to unknown_bundle. Make the resolver accept an INJECTABLE lookup fn (default = bundleByKey) so the happy path is exercisable in tests without setting POLAR_PRODUCT_* env. Keeps the extraction behavior-preserving while making both the success and 400 paths testable.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 11:58 UTC
Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 11:58 UTC
ai-bookkeeper versioning: pre-1.0, MINOR-per-feature cadence (v0.10.0 decision)
content: ai-bookkeeper (xkazm04/xprize-ai-bookkeeper) is pre-1.0 and its CHANGELOG explicitly states that while 0.x, even breaking changes increment the MINOR version. Release cadence has been steady MINOR bumps: v0.3.0→...→v0.9.0, one feature-batch per release. v0.10.0 decision (2026-06-01): bumped from 0.9.0, MINOR, for commit 6034931e ('metered AI advisor tier, multi-env DB + dev-auth, Claude provider, e2e suite') — purely additive (new /api/ask + /api/insights/* routes, src/lib/db driver abstraction, Claude CLI provider, DEV_AUTH, Playwright e2e). No breaking API removals. Process note: team policy has Automerge DISABLED and requires PRs against main — so releases are staged as a release/vX.Y.0 PR (version bump + changelog) and the tag/publish is held for human approval rather than committed straight to main. Carry forward: next default bump is MINOR unless commits are pure fixes (PATCH) or a 1.0 line is declared.
